response: I understand you've lost access to your Claude team workspace and need it restored. Unfortunately, seat management — including restoring access to an unassigned member — is an admin-level action that cannot be performed by non-admin users. Only your workspace owner or IT admin can restore your access. Here's what they need to do: 1. Go to Organization settings > Organization. Find your name in the member list. Click the dropdown under Seat Type. Select "Enterprise seat" (or the appropriate seat type for your plan). As noted in the documentation, "Unassigned members remain part of your organization but cannot use Claude until they're assigned to a seat." This means your account still exists in the organization — you just need a seat reassigned to you. What you should do now: 1. Contact your IT admin or workspace owner directly and ask them to reassign you to a seat using the steps above. If you're unsure who your admin is or cannot reach them, you'll need to escalate through your internal company channels (such as your IT helpdesk) to get the seat restored. I'm unable to restore your access directly, as seat assignments must be managed by someone with admin privileges on your organization's workspace. For account-specific actions like this, you'll need to work with your admin team or contact human support if your organization is unresponsive. Sources: Purchase and manage seats on Enterprise plans / Temporarily remove a member's access

Sources: End an Interview Ending an Interview / Ending an Interview; Viewing a Candidate's Detailed Test Report Prerequisite Viewing Detailed Reports Key Features of a Detailed Report / Code Playback; Interview Integrity Signals Enabling Interview Integrity Signals How it works Types of suspicious signals / Types of suspicious signals

Issue 2: The issue is likely caused by the system interpreting interviewer inactivity on the HR interface as the interviewer having left the session. If the interviewer is the only other participant besides the candidate, their perceived inactivity can trigger the automatic move to the lobby. According to the "End an Interview" documentation, the system behavior is as follows: * "If no other interviewers are present, the candidate moves to the lobby, and the interview ends automatically after an hour of inactivity." While this describes an hour-long inactivity timeout, the core mechanism of moving the candidate to the lobby when the system believes the interviewer has left may be what's occurring. The interviewer's focus on observing the screen share, rather than interacting with the HR controls, could be causing the platform to flag them as inactive. The documentation does not explicitly detail activity detection mechanics for interviewers during screen sharing or Observation Mode, nor does it provide a setting to adjust this behavior. The available articles focus on features like Observation Mode for monitoring and image proctoring for candidates, but not on preserving the interview session when an interviewer is passively observing. The documentation does not fully cover this specific case. A human support agent should review to confirm the exact inactivity timeout settings for interviewer screens and explore if there is a configuration to prevent the lobby redirect during active

Sources: End an Interview Ending an Interview / Ending an Interview; April 2026 Release Notes Screen Interview Platform Chakra (AI Interviewer) SkillUp Community Deprecations and Experience Changes / HackerRank Desktop App improvements; Best Practices to Maintain Test Integrity / Image Proctoring

Issue 3: The documentation does not provide a way to extend the inactivity timeout for interviews; it is set to one hour and ends the interview automatically if no other interviewers are present. According to the "End an Interview" documentation: "If no other interviewers are present, the candidate moves to the lobby, and the interview ends automatically after an hour of inactivity." There are no settings mentioned to adjust or extend this inactivity period for live interviews. For test duration, you can add time accommodation before or after sending invites if the candidate has not started the test, but this does not apply to interview inactivity. Since the documentation does not cover modifying inactivity times for interviews, you may need to contact human support for assistance or to request this feature. Sources: End an Interview, Extend Test Duration for Candidates Add Time Accommodation Add extra time

response: Claude is likely experiencing high system-wide demand causing temporary capacity constraints, which is preventing responses. This is not a permanent outage. If the issue persists for an extended period, it may be a different problem. The documentation does not provide further troubleshooting steps for a complete failure to respond beyond waiting. response: To block Anthropic's crawler (ClaudeBot) from your website, you need to modify your site's robots.txt file. Locate the robots.txt file in the top-level directory of your website (e.g., 2. Add the following entries to block crawling across your entire site: User-agent: ClaudeBot Disallow: / 3. If you want to limit rather than fully block crawling, you can set a crawl delay using the non-standard Crawl-delay extension: User-agent: ClaudeBot Crawl-delay: 1 4. Repeat this for every subdomain you want to opt out from crawling. Important notes: - Blocking IP addresses that Anthropic's crawler uses will not reliably work as an alternative. IP-based blocking may prevent Anthropic from reading your robots.txt file, which means your opt-out may not persist. Modifying robots.txt is the recommended and supported method. - To verify a crawler is from Anthropic, the source IP address should appear in Anthropic's published list of bot IPs at the URL referenced in their crawling documentation. If you have already updated your robots.txt and believe your content is still being crawled, or if you need to request removal of specific content that has already been indexed, you can submit a report through Anthropic's content reporting process for further assistance. Sources: Does Anthropic crawl data from the web, and how can site owners block the crawler?

response:
All requests to Claude via AWS Bedrock failing typically indicates either a credentials issue or a model access/region configuration issue. Here are the steps to troubleshoot: 1. Verify your AWS credentials are working correctly by running `aws sts get-caller-identity` from the command line. If this fails, your AWS credentials are not properly configured. Confirm the `AWSREGION` environment variable is set to a region where your requested Claude model is enabled. Not all Claude models are available in every Bedrock region. Check that the specific Claude model you are requesting is enabled in your Bedrock console for that region. You can verify this in the AWS Bedrock console under "Model access." 4. If using Claude Code, you can quickly test by running `/model` and selecting a model you know you have access to, to isolate whether the issue is model-specific. If you are using Claude Code with Vertex instead, confirm that `gcloud auth application-default login` has been run and that `ANTHROPICVERTEXPROJECTID` and `CLOUDMLREGION` are set correctly. If none of these steps resolve the issue, you will need to contact AWS support directly, since Claude usage through Bedrock is managed by AWS. Anthropic cannot access or modify your Bedrock configuration, credentials, or billing. For billing or refund inquiries related to Bedrock usage, those are handled through AWS and are non-refundable per Anthropic's policy unless you have a private offer and direct contract with Anthropic. response: To remove a former employee from your HackerRank hiring account, you must request deletion through HackerRank support, as there is no direct option in the settings to remove a user from your organization. Steps to Request Deletion: 1. Submit a request by emailing support@hackerrank.com or by using the Submit a Request form. You will need to log in to the HackerRank platform to use the form. In your request, specify that you need to delete data for a former employee or candidate. You must provide the relevant list of candidates to be deleted. Alternatively, if you have an assigned Customer Success Manager or Account Manager, you can route the request through them. Important Details: Only company admins have the ability to delete individual candidate profiles directly from the platform. For removing a former team member from your organization's hiring account, a support request is the required process. Once HackerRank receives your request and the list of individuals, they will anonymize the data within 14 days and notify you upon completion. * Deleted data cannot be reinstated. If you need to delete your own personal HackerRank Community account, you can do so via the Settings page by scrolling to the "Delete Accounts" section. This is separate from managing your organization's hiring account. Sources: Delete an Account, GDPR FAQs for HackerRank for Work

To set up the Claude LTI for your students in Canvas, you need to complete three main steps: enable the integration in your Claude for Education organization settings, create a developer key in Canvas, and install the LTI app. Here is the full process: Step 1: Enable the Claude LTI Integration in Claude for Education 1. Sign in to Claude for Education as an administrator. Navigate to Organization settings > Connectors. Find Canvas and click "Enable." 4. In the settings modal, enter the following: - Canvas Domain - Client ID (you will get this from Step 2 below) - Deployment ID (found in Canvas Admin -> Settings -> Apps -> View App Configurations -> Claude LTI Settings Button -> Deployment ID) 5. Click "Save Changes." Step 2: Create a Claude LTI Developer Key in Canvas 1. In Canvas, sign in as an administrator and navigate to Admin -> Developer Keys. Click "+ Developer Key" then "+ LTI Key." 3. Enter the following information: - Key Name: Claude LTI - Description: Enter a short description for the LTI 1.3 app - Redirect URIs: - Title: Claude LTI - Target Link URI: - OpenID Connect Initiation Url: - JWK method: 4. Under Additional Settings, toggle Privacy Level to Public. Under Placements, we recommend removing the defaults and adding "Course Navigation" and "Assignment Edit" as the options. Save the key and note the Client ID it generates — you will need this to complete Step 1. Step 3: Install Claude LTI as an App in Canvas 1. In Canvas, go to Admin -> Settings -> Apps.

Sources: Set up the Claude LTI in Canvas by Instructure / Turn on the Claude LTI Integration in Claude for Education organization settings; Set up the Claude LTI in Canvas by Instructure / Questions; Set up the Claude LTI in Canvas by Instructure / Installing Claude LTI as an App

response: Yes, merchants in the US Virgin Islands are permitted to set a minimum transaction amount of US$10 for Visa credit card payments. According to Visa's policy, in general, a merchant is not permitted to establish a minimum or maximum amount for a Visa transaction. However, there is a specific exception for the USA and its territories, which includes the US Virgin Islands. In these locations, and only for credit cards, a merchant may require a minimum transaction amount of US$10. If you are using a Visa debit card, this minimum does not apply, and the merchant should not be refusing the transaction based on a minimum amount. If you believe the merchant is not following these rules, you should notify your Visa card issuer. Sources: Visa Consumer Support / Can a merchant set a maximum or minimum limit for using my Visa card?
